node.statement: (Real vs complex subtlety). For the real PSD cone S^n_+ (real symmetric matrices), the classical BP bound is r(r+1)/2 ≤ m, which is strictly better than r² ≤ m. This is because faces of S^n_+ have dimension r(r+1)/2 (the dimension of the real symmetric r×r matrices), while faces of H_n^+ have dimension r² (the dimension of the Hermitian r×r matrices). Our theorem, working with *-algebras over C, naturally gives the Hermitian/complex version. For real *-algebras (with trivial involution, i.e., A = M_n(R)^{sa}), the commutant analysis would yield π(A)'^{sa} having the dimension of real symmetric matrices, r(r+1)/2, recovering the tighter real bound. The real case requires working with Jordan algebras or real order unit spaces rather than complex *-algebras.
